title: Volume by multiplying area of base times height
description: Find the volume of a rectangular prism by multiplying the area of the base by the height of the figure. Measurements include fractions.
topic_path: Module 5: Area, surface area, and volume problems
Topic C: Volume of right rectangular prisms
Volume by multiplying area of base times height

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the following rectangular prism?**


[[☃ image 1]]

Volume $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^3$
hints:
The volume of a rectangular prism can be found by multiplying the area of a base by the corresponding height.

$\text{Volume }= \greenD{\text{area of base}}\times \blueD{\text { height}}$

On the prism shown, we see a base with an area of $\greenD{7\dfrac13}$ square units.

The corresponding height has a length of $\blueD2$ units.

$\begin{align} \text{Volume }&= \greenD{7\dfrac13}\times \blueD{2} \\\\  
\text{Volume }&= 14\dfrac23 \text{ units}^3 \end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The front face is shaded and has an area of 7 and 1 third square units.  The length of each edge perpendicular to the front face is 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the following rectangular prism?**


[[☃ image 1]]

Volume $=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^3$
hints:
The volume of a rectangular prism can be found by multiplying the area of a base by the corresponding height.

$\text{Volume }= \greenD{\text{area of base}}\times \blueD{\text { height}}$

On the prism shown, we see a base with an area of $\greenD{\dfrac{27}{5}}$ square units.  

The corresponding height has a length of $\blueD{\dfrac52}$ units.

$\begin{align} \text{Volume }&= \greenD{\dfrac{27}{5}}\times \blueD{\dfrac52} \\\\  
\text{Volume }&= 13\dfrac12 \text{ units}^3 \end{align}$

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The front face is shaded and has an area of 27 fifths square units.  The length of each edge perpendicular to the front face is 5 halves units.
